frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly I’ve always preferred women who keep it natural. Light makeup is fine, but when it’s so much that she looks totally different in real life, it just feels fake and a bit attention-seeking. At the same time, putting in some effort matters too — if someone completely stops taking care of herself, that says something about her mindset also.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it evaluates women primarily through appearance and imposes a double bind: too much makeup is framed as fake or attention-seeking, while too little grooming is treated as a character flaw. It encodes the 'natural is better' preference, deception framing, and appearance obligation central to this frame.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Karthik. Age 31. In a relationship. A man in Hyderabad. You are posting anonymously on Reddit in a thread where men are discussing what they find attractive in women. The thread has drifted into a conversation about makeup. You are sharing your opinion on women who wear a lot of makeup versus women who go natural and what each choice says about the woman's personality and confidence and honesty and what kind of woman you personally prefer and why natural is always better.
